bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening since there is no time pressure yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me low utility overall, with concessions only on issueD and issueE, so I will keep issueD as valueB since it is equally best for me while holding firm on the other high-value issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer for me rather than conceding, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. To avoid simply repeating my last bid while preserving my utility, I switch issueD from valueB to valueA, which is equally preferred by me.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than making costly concessions. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and show little/no concession, but they previously offered issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA, so I can choose issueD:valueB at no utility cost to me while holding firm on my important issues.

At t=0.06 this is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than making meaningful concessions. The opponent has mostly held firm on low-utility values for issueA/B/C/F, with only issueD moving among acceptable values, so I will match their latest issueD=valueA while holding my high-weight priorities.

I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession; their offers remain very low for me, especially on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF. Since issueD has equal utility for me at valueA or valueB, I can switch it to valueB to avoid repeating my exact previous bid while preserving full utility.

I am targeting maximum utility (1.00) because it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded toward my high-value issues. Their offers consistently demand my low-utility values on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, while only varying issueD; with no messages, I rely on the bidding pattern and hold firm for now while avoiding repeating my immediately previous bid exactly.

I am targeting maximum utility because it is still very early (t=0.12) and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded on the high-weight issues A, B, and C. Their bids consistently give me very low values except issueE and sometimes issueD, so I will keep my core preferences while choosing issueD: valueB, which is equally valuable to me and may align better with some of their prior offers.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on a low-weight issue. The opponent has held firm on issueA/issueB/issueC as valueC and issueF as valueC, while accepting issueE valueA and often issueD valueB, so I will preserve my high-weight priorities and offer a modest move on issueF.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a small concession to keep momentum. The opponent’s latest offer improved substantially for me by moving issueA to valueA, but they continue to hold issueB, issueC, and issueF at valueC, so I will hold my high-weight priorities while conceding on lower-weight issueF.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent has just made meaningful concessions on issueA and issueC. Their latest offer is already fairly good for me except issueB and issueF; I will keep the concession on issueF but continue pressing for a better issueB while varying issueD at no cost to my utility.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.20, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has improved substantially on issueA and issueC but remains firm on issueB and issueF; I will concede issueF and set issueD to a value I am indifferent about while holding firm on high-value issueB and issueC.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 while making only a small concession on low-weight issueF. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE, but remains difficult on issueC and issueF; I will hold firm on high-weight issueC and vary issueD, which is equally good for me.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid, conceding mainly on issueF and partially on issueC while holding issueA and issueB where the opponent has recently moved toward my preferred values. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I do not need to accept their latest low issueC/issueF combination outright, but their repeated offers suggest issueF:valueC and issueD:valueB are acceptable tradeoffs.

At t=0.26 it is still early enough to target a high-utility bid for me, while making concessions on issues the opponent has repeatedly signaled, especially issueF=valueC and issueD=valueB. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially at times, including offering issueA=valueA, issueB=valueB, and issueE=valueA, though their latest offer backslid on issueB and issueD, so I am countering with a strong but plausible compromise rather than conceding further.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.84, since we are only at t=0.28 and there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent has begun conceding on issueA and sometimes issueB/issueC, while consistently leaving issueE at my best value and pushing issueF to valueC, so I concede on issueF and partially on issueC while holding issueA/issueB/issueD/issueE favorable.

At t=0.30 I am still targeting a strong utility bid, but I am conceding on issueF since the opponent has consistently kept it at valueC and it is only 0.1 weight for me. The opponent’s latest valid offer has improved for me on issueA and issueB but still pushes issueC to my worst value, so I am holding issueC at valueA while keeping their repeated preferences on issueD/valueB and issueF/valueC.

I am targeting about 0.92 utility: strong on the high-weight issues while still conceding issueF to valueC, which the opponent has consistently demanded. At t=0.32 there is some time pressure but not enough to make a large concession; the opponent’s offers have improved on issueA and issueC, though they still often hold issueB/issueF low for me. I vary from my immediately previous bid by using issueD:valueA, which is utility-equivalent for me to valueB.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because we are still in the early-mid phase at t=0.34, so there is no need for a large concession yet. The opponent has gradually conceded on issueA and sometimes issueB/issueC, while consistently pushing issueF: valueC and often using issueD: valueB, so I concede on issueF and choose issueD: valueB since it costs me nothing versus valueA. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 since it is still only mid-negotiation at t=0.36, while maintaining the major concession on issueF that the opponent consistently demands. The opponent has conceded on issueA and sometimes issueC/issueB, but remains firm on issueF=valueC and issueE=valueA; with no messages to weigh, I am trusting the offer pattern and keeping my high-weight issues mostly favorable.

At t=0.38 we are still in the mid-game, so I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid rather than accepting the opponent’s low issueC position. The opponent has clearly conceded on issueA and sometimes issueB, while consistently pushing issueF=valueC; I can concede issueF and partially concede issueC to valueB while holding the high-value positions on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.92 by holding the high-weight issues where the opponent has already shown willingness to concede, especially issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.40 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their latest 0.76-utility bid; their history shows movement toward my preferred issueA/issueB values while they consistently prefer issueF:valueC, which I can concede.

I am targeting a high but plausible utility around 0.92, since we are still before the late phase and the opponent’s latest offer already gives me strong values on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE. Their behavior suggests they strongly prefer issueF: valueC and can sometimes accept issueB: valueB, so I will hold firm on the important issueB while keeping concessions they have repeatedly signaled.

I am targeting a strong but more negotiable utility of about 0.844 rather than staying at my maximum, since we are in the mid-game at t=0.44 but not yet under deadline pressure. The opponent has consistently pushed issueF to valueC while keeping issueE at valueA, and has sometimes offered issueB=valueB and issueD=valueB, so I concede issueF and partially concede issueC while holding the most valuable positions on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE.

At t=0.46 I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid around 0.84, since we are in the mid-game and there is still time to avoid over-conceding. The opponent has repeatedly signaled acceptance of issueA=valueA, issueB=valueB, issueD=valueB, and issueE=valueA, while holding firm on issueF=valueC and pushing issueC down; I concede issueF and partially concede issueC to valueB rather than valueC.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.84, conceding on lower-cost areas rather than giving up the most important high-utility positions too quickly. The opponent has consistently demanded issueF: valueC and often pushes issueC downward, while they have already shown willingness to accept issueA: valueA, issueE: valueA, and sometimes issueB: valueB, so I keep issueB firm and offer a moderate concession on issueC and issueF. At t=0.48 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to their latest low-utility offer.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 by holding firm on the important issues where the opponent has already shown willingness to concede, especially issueA and issueC, while giving them issueF:valueC which they consistently request. At t=0.50 there is moderate time pressure, so I am making a practical concession on issueF and choosing issueD:valueB, which is fully valuable to me and appears more acceptable to them than issueD:valueA. The opponent’s offers have improved from very low utility to around 0.60 for me, but they are still holding issueB and issueF hard, so this bid tests whether they will accept a near-compromise without me conceding issueB.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.84 by holding firm on high-value issueA/issueB and keeping mutually favorable issueD/issueE, while conceding issueF where the opponent has consistently insisted on valueC. The opponent’s latest valid offer is worth about 0.76 to me and shows some movement on issueA and issueB, but they still push issueC down to valueC, so I am offering issueC: valueB as a compromise rather than fully accepting their low value.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.84 while still making meaningful concessions on issueF and a compromise on issueC. The opponent has consistently pushed issueF to valueC and often issueC to valueC, while accepting our preferred issueA/issueB more often lately, so I will concede on issueF and keep issueC at valueB rather than dropping fully to valueC at t=0.54.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.92, keeping my important issues B and C at top values while already conceding issueF to valueC, which the opponent consistently demands and is lower weight for me. At t=0.56 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their low-B proposal; their recent offers show willingness to give issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE favorable values, while still pressing on issueB and issueF.

At t=0.58 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.84 while making a meaningful mid-game concession to improve acceptability. The opponent has consistently signaled issueE:valueA and issueF:valueC, has conceded issueA to valueA and sometimes issueB to valueB, but remains difficult on issueC, so I concede issueC only to valueB rather than valueC while holding my high-value positions elsewhere.

I am targeting a high but more flexible utility around 0.894 by conceding issueF to valueC, which the opponent has consistently demanded, while keeping my high-value outcomes on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE. At t=0.60 there is some time pressure to move beyond repeated bids, and the opponent’s recent offers suggest they strongly prefer issueB=valueC but have also used issueB=valueB, so I am trying issueB=valueA as a middle concession rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a high but realistic utility around 0.92, since we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent has repeatedly signaled acceptance of issueA=valueA, issueE=valueA, issueF=valueC, and has sometimes offered issueB=valueB, issueC=valueA, and issueD=valueB, so this bid combines their better concessions while holding my important issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.844 while making a meaningful mid/late-game concession from my top positions. The opponent has repeatedly conceded issueA to valueA and sometimes offered issueB valueB / issueD valueB, but they keep holding issueF at valueC, so I concede issueF and partially concede issueC while protecting the higher-value issues as much as possible. There are no messages to weigh, and all observed offers use valid domain values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 by keeping my important gains on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD while conceding the lower-weight issueF to valueC, which the opponent consistently asks for. At t=0.66 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their latest 0.76-utility offer; their bids show real concessions on issueA and issueC, but they still often hold issueB at valueC, so I am countering with valueB there. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.76 while making a meaningful concession as the negotiation moves into the later mid-game. The opponent has consistently pushed issueF to valueC and often issueB to valueC, while accepting issueA valueA, issueC valueA, issueE valueA, and sometimes issueD valueB, so I concede issueB and issueF but hold stronger positions elsewhere.
